title: Classic Eggs Benedict
description: A paleo twist on the breakfast classic with creamy hollandaise over perfectly poached eggs and savory beef patties instead of English muffins.
instructions: Form ground beef into 4 thin patties, season with salt and pepper, and cook in a skillet over medium-high heat until done, about 3-4 minutes per side. Keep warm.
For the hollandaise, blend egg yolks and lemon juice in a blender for 30 seconds. With blender running on low, slowly drizzle in the melted ghee until sauce thickens. Add salt and cayenne.

Fill a large pot with water, add vinegar, and bring to a gentle simmer. Create a gentle whirlpool and carefully drop in eggs one at a time. Poach for 3-4 minutes until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Place beef patties on plates, top each with 2 poached eggs, and drizzle generously with hollandaise sauce.
Garnish with fresh chives and serve immediately.

title: Sweet Potato Hash with Ground Beef
description: A hearty breakfast hash featuring crispy sweet potatoes, seasoned ground beef, and colorful bell peppers. Perfect for meal prep.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of avocado oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add diced sweet potatoes in a single layer. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and crispy on the edges.
Push sweet potatoes to one side, add remaining oil, then add ground beef. Break it up and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add bell peppers, onion, and garlic. Season with paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Mix everything together, taste and adjust seasonings. Garnish with fresh parsley.
Serve hot, optionally topped with fried eggs.

title: 3-Ingredient Banana Pancakes
description: Simple, fluffy pancakes made with just bananas, eggs, and almond butter. Naturally sweet and incredibly satisfying.
instructions: Mash the bananas in a bowl until smooth. Add eggs and almond butter, whisk until well combined. Stir in cinnamon if using.
Heat coconut oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour about 2 tablespoons of batter per pancake. Cook for 2-3 minutes until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set.
Carefully flip and cook for another 1-2 minutes until golden brown.
Serve warm topped with fresh berries and a drizzle of almond butter.

title: Veggie-Packed Frittata
description: A protein-rich frittata loaded with colorful vegetables. Perfect for breakfast, brunch, or even dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Whisk eggs with salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning in a large bowl. Set aside.
Heat olive oil in a 10-inch oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 3 minutes until softened.
Add zucchini, bell pepper, and garlic. Cook for 4-5 minutes until vegetables begin to soften.
Add spinach and stir until wilted, about 1 minute. Scatter cherry tomatoes over the vegetables.
Pour egg mixture evenly over the vegetables. Cook without stirring for 2-3 minutes until edges begin to set.
Transfer skillet to oven and bake for 15-18 minutes until eggs are set and top is lightly golden.
Let cool for 5 minutes, garnish with fresh basil, slice and serve.

title: Coconut Chia Pudding
description: Creamy, make-ahead chia pudding with coconut milk and a hint of vanilla. Top with your favorite fruits and nuts.
instructions: In a large bowl or jar, combine chia seeds, coconut milk, vanilla extract, honey, and cinnamon. Whisk well to combine.
Let sit for 5 minutes, then whisk again to prevent clumping.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until thick and pudding-like.
Stir before serving. Divide into bowls or jars.
Top with fresh berries, sliced almonds, and coconut flakes.
Store in refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: Sausage & Egg Breakfast Muffins
description: Portable protein-packed egg cups with ground sausage and vegetables. Make a batch Sunday, reheat all week.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a 12-cup muffin tin with avocado oil.
Cook ground sausage in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up, until browned through, about 6-7 minutes. Drain any excess fat.
Add spinach to the skillet and stir until wilted, about 1 minute. Remove from heat.
Whisk eggs with garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Divide sausage mixture, diced bell pepper, and green onions evenly among the muffin cups.
Pour egg mixture over the fillings, filling each cup about three-quarters full.
Bake for 20-22 minutes until eggs are set and tops are lightly golden.
Let cool in the tin for 5 minutes before removing. Store in the fridge up to 5 days or freeze for up to 2 months.

title: Turkey Apple Breakfast Skillet
description: Savory ground turkey with diced apples, sage, and sweet potatoes. A hearty one-pan breakfast with the perfect sweet-savory balance.
instructions: Heat avocado oil in a large cast iron skillet over medium heat. Add diced sweet potato and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender and starting to crisp.
Push sweet potatoes to the edges and add ground turkey to the center. Break up and cook until no longer pink, about 5 minutes.
Add onion, apple, sage, cinnamon, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Stir everything together.
Cook for 4-5 minutes until apples are just tender and onion is translucent.
Taste and adjust seasonings. Serve hot, optionally topped with fried eggs.
